ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a chicken-mushroom melt sandwich meal set. FOODON_00002473 (sandwich food product) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKK (Sandwich) is the closest parent. No specific code exists for a chicken-mushroom melt meal set with cookie.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code exists for this specific commercial sandwich meal set product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard meal set consisting of a 6-inch chicken and mushroom melt submarine sandwich (approximately 250 g) plus one Subway-style cookie (approximately 70 g), totalling roughly 320 g per serving.

nutrient_source.title: Subway Chicken & Bacon Ranch Melt 6-inch and Subway Cookies — USDA Branded Food / Subway nutritional data adapted for Chicken Mushroom Melt Meal composite
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (Branded Foods) — Subway entries; supplemented by USDA SR Legacy for mushroom and cheese components
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA or HPB entry exists for 'Chicken and Mushroom Melt Cookie Meal' as a composite. Nutrient values are estimated by combining: (1) USDA Branded Food entry for Subway 6-inch Chicken sub (FDC ID 2097663, ~250 g) providing chicken, bread, cheese, and vegetable components; (2) USDA Branded Food entry for Subway Chocolate Chip Cookie (~70 g, FDC ID 2097700). Per-100g values are derived from the weighted composite of the 320 g meal set. Mushroom contribution estimated from USDA SR Legacy item 'Mushrooms, white, raw' (FDC ID 169251). All values are estimates; confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of bread-based sandwich meals — analogous estimate based on white/mixed-grain submarine roll GI data; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC (2008) International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a Chicken and Mushroom Melt Cookie Meal. GI of 55 is estimated as a weighted composite: the 6-inch submarine roll component uses GI ~55 (white/mixed-grain sub roll, University of Sydney GI Database); the cookie component uses GI ~57 (oatmeal cookie, Atkinson et al. 2008 International Tables). Weighted average across 320 g serving yields composite GI ~55, which is classified as low. Glycemic load per serving = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (55 × 107.2) / 100 ≈ 59.2. Carbohydrate per serving = 33.5 g/100g × 320 g = 107.2 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~480 mg per 100 g; ~1536 mg per meal set serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this meal set, as a single serving provides approximately 1536 mg sodium — exceeding two-thirds of the WHO recommended daily limit of 2000 mg — and should request reduced-sodium modifications such as omitting cheese and sauces where possible.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load per serving (~59) driven by refined carbohydrate from the sub roll and cookie
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should exercise caution with this meal set due to its high glycemic load of approximately 59 per serving, which can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation; choosing a wholegrain bread option, removing the cookie, and pairing with non-starchy vegetables may help moderate the glycaemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat (~3.1 g per 100 g) and high sodium (~480 mg per 100 g) with processed cheese and cookie components
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals with cardiovascular disease, the combination of saturated fat from melted cheese and high sodium from the sub and sauces warrants portion control; selecting a lower-fat protein option, omitting the cookie, and requesting no added sauces can reduce saturated fat and sodium intake meaningfully.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Chicken and Mushroom Melt Cookie Meal is a Western fast-casual sandwich meal set sold in Singapore through outlets such as Subway and similar mix-and-match sandwich counters. The core component is a 6-inch submarine sandwich filled with seasoned chicken breast strips, sautéed or marinated mushrooms, and melted cheese — typically processed American-style cheese — layered on a soft white or mixed-grain sub roll and topped with fresh vegetables such as lettuce, tomato, and onion. The sandwich is typically toasted to melt the cheese and warm the filling, producing a soft, slightly crispy bread exterior with a savoury, umami-rich interior. The meal set is completed with a cookie — commonly a chocolate chip or oatmeal raisin variety — adding a sweet, buttery finish. Nutritionally, the meal is energy-dense at approximately 248 kcal per 100 g, with a moderate protein content (~12.5 g/100 g) from the chicken, significant carbohydrate (~33.5 g/100 g) from the bread and cookie, and moderate fat (~7.8 g/100 g) including saturated fat from the cheese. Sodium is notably high (~480 mg/100 g), making the full meal set a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic load per serving is high (~59), driven by the refined carbohydrate content of the sub roll and cookie, which is clinically relevant for Type 2 Diabetes management. The meal is widely available on field survey data and is priced at approximately SGD 14.50. It is suitable for halal diets but contains gluten, dairy, and egg allergens.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for 'Chicken and Mushroom Melt Cookie Meal' exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or any other approved primary source as a composite meal set. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA Branded Food entries for Subway sub and cookie components, weighted by serving mass. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database data for sub rolls and cookies. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component-level USDA data is high quality and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: 1. Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA Branded Food source entries used for this composite estimation. 2. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA Branded Food source entries used for this composite estimation. 3. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA Branded Food source entries used for this composite estimation. 4. The food_group in the input JSON was listed as 'Vegetables', which is incorrect for a sandwich meal set; corrected to 'Mixed' per Veritas classification rules. 5. The halal flag is set to true based on Subway Singapore's halal certification status; individual outlet certification should be verified. 6. GI is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this specific meal set was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. 7. Serving size of 320 g is an estimate based on a standard 6-inch Subway sub (~250 g) plus one cookie (~70 g). 8. Price preserved from input: SGD 14.50 (min and max identical, single price point across 2 restaurants).

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Corrected food_group from 'Vegetables' (input error) to 'Mixed'. Added all mandatory Singaporean multilingual local names (Chinese simplified/traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi). Built composite nutrient estimate from USDA Branded Food Subway entries. Documented all -1 sentinel values (vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, selenium_mcg) in notes. Set GI to 55 (medium) as analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database. Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases consistency. Set ontology match types to 'closest_parent' or 'none' only — no invented codes.

Iteration 2: Corrected gi_category from 'medium' to 'low' for glycemic_index 55, as per standard GI classification. Updated gi_source.source_match_notes to reflect this change. This resolves the q4_glycemic_completeness failure from the previous iteration.

Iteration 3: Verified all hard gates pass. Confirmed score_total = 8/8. Confirmed zero-macro justification rule not triggered (no core macros are zero). Confirmed sentinel value documentation rule satisfied. Confirmed aliases consistency rule satisfied. Set final_decision to PASS.
